channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Signal: misusing a language feature
item.description: I saw a new library, Signal were they are using PHP attributes as documentation. The idea on itself just blew my mind, because PHP attributes are the solution to keep metadata out of the comments....

item.title: Why Every Frontier Model Gets Worse After Launch — The Regression Trap
item.description: The r/ClaudeCode thread hit 1,700 upvotes in 48 hours. Developers called it "Gaslightus 4.7" — a model that invents files, defends hallucinated results across ten turns, and exhausts your Pro plan...

item.title: How to Build a Reusable TextInput Component in React with TypeScript
item.description: Introduction I was copy-pasting the same markup everywhere, tweaking a class name here, adding an error message there. In my Smart Budget Tracker app, I decided to create a reusable TextInput...

item.title: Automate Deployment Testing | 🏗️ Build A Version Management System
item.description: Exam Guide: Developer - Associate 🏗️ Domain 3: Deployment 📘 Task 3: Automate Deployment Testing This task is about automating tests as part of your deployment workflow: managing Lambda versions...

item.title: The True Classification of AI: Functional AI
item.description: This is part of a larger classification framework I’m publishing piece by piece. The order will make sense once the full series is up. People talk about AI like it’s one giant, mysterious, semi...

item.title: Building a Browser-Based Image Resizer with Step-Down Scaling and Crop
item.description: Building a Browser-Based Image Resizer Image resizing sounds trivial — scale the width, scale the height, done. But there's a surprising amount of detail involved in making it work well across...

item.title: OpenClaw: 210K Stars in 4 Months — Local-First AI Agent Deep Dive
item.description: 9,000 stars to 210,000 in four months. That number is not a typo, and it did not happen because of a viral tweet. OpenClaw — built on NousResearch's Hermes Agent stack — crossed 210,000 GitHub...

item.title: How I Built a Free Financial Calculator for 5 Countries (and what I learned about personal finance along the way)
item.description: When I started building Wealth Calculator Hub, I had one specific problem: I couldn't find a single financial calculator that worked correctly for the country I was actually dealing with. Every...

item.title: My AI memory benchmark said 98.3%. The number was true — and worthless.
item.description: In my last post I introduced Bastra Recall — an MIT-licensed MCP memory server that gives Claude persistent memory as plain Markdown in a local Obsidian vault. I promised a follow-up on retrieval...

item.title: I Built a 30-Line Chrome Extension to Kill the YouTube Feed
item.description: I open youtube.com to grab one specific video, and forty minutes later I'm three recommendations deep into something I never went there for. The homepage feed is one of the platform's most...

item.title: Qi fan fan
item.description: Despite my initial skepticism, I'm now sold on wireless Qi chargers that add integrated fans to keep your phone cool while charging. I figured they'd be too loud, or too weak, or too gimmicky, but...

item.title: The only AI glossary you’ll need this year
item.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

item.title: Unpatched Flaws Disclosed in Filesystem Bundled Into Millions of Embedded Devices
item.description: Security firm runZero has disclosed seven vulnerabilities in FatFs, a small filesystem library that lets a device read and write the FAT and exFAT formats used on USB drives and SD cards. The...

item.title: New "Bad Epoll" Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Unprivileged Users Gain Root, Hits Android
item.description: A newly disclosed Linux kernel flaw called Bad Epoll (CVE-2026-46242) lets an ordinary user with no special access take full control of a machine as root. It affects Linux desktops, servers, and...

item.title: New Avalon Malware Framework Packs CrownX Ransomware Capabilities
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented modular malware framework codenamed Avalon that's distributed by means of a multi-stage phishing chain capable of bypassing...

item.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
item.description: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

item.title: A device that revives eyeballs from dead donors could make eye transplants possible
item.description: It’s not easy to transplant a whole human eye. The surgery is difficult. And the eyes themselves start to degenerate as soon as they’ve left the body. When surgeons attempted it a few years ago,...

item.title: Amazon updated 2023’s Fire HD 10 tablet with 4GB of RAM
item.description: The Fire HD 8 that launched in 2024 was the last new addition to Amazon's budget-minded tablet lineup, but the company has quietly updated the Fire HD 10 that debuted the year before. In 2023 it...

item.title: Google DeepMind Unionization Talks Are Off to a Rocky Start
item.description: During negotiations on Wednesday, employees voiced frustrations with what they consider an unwillingness among executives to engage meaningfully with the prospect of unionization.

item.title: North Korea-Linked npm Packages Mimic Rollup Polyfills to Steal Developer Secrets
item.description: Threat actors with ties to North Korea have been linked to a fresh set of malicious npm packages that masquerade as Rollup polyfill tooling to facilitate remote access and data theft. According to...

item.title: While you’re watching the World Cup, the feds may be watching you
item.description: It's a big year for America. It's the semiquincentennial, otherwise known as America250, and the United States is cohosting the World Cup. But spectators at these events - and the millions of...

item.title: This slim camera has a transparent LCD screen for a viewfinder
item.description: Despite the fact that smartphones have become impressively capable shooters, standalone point-and-shoot cameras are enjoying a renaissance. The tiny Kodak Charmera is still wildly popular, while...

item.title: I finally got my Trump phone
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. One year on, our phones have finally arrived. 12 months, 16 days, 21 hours, and 54 minutes after I first heard about Trump...

item.title: Hydration isn’t complicated: Just drink water
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Opt in...

item.title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
item.description: At the event "The Briefing: AI for Science" earlier this week, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a new "AI workbench for scientists" that pulls fragmented tools and datasets into one...

item.title: Armored Likho Targets Government Agencies, Power Sector with BusySnake Stealer
item.description: A previously undocumented threat actor known as Armored Likho has been attributed to cyber attacks targeting government agencies and the electric power sector across Russia, Brazil, and...

item.title: Jon Prosser responds to Apple lawsuit by blaming the other guy
item.description: YouTuber Jon Prosser has finally filed a formal response to Apple's lawsuit made against him and another defendant over allegedly stealing iOS secrets. In his response, Prosser denied that he...

item.title: The Verge’s annual summer ‘in’ and ‘out’ list
item.description: In the AI slop-loaded, algorithm-powered modern reality, trends come and go - and the tech industry is no different. For the last few years, The Verge staff has compiled a selection of things that...

item.title: The Download: a smoking “endgame” and a new Elizabeth Bear story
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work. I’m...

item.title: Apple TV is hitting its stride
item.description: Since its inception, Apple TV, née Apple TV Plus, has built a reputation on quality over quantity. It has far fewer shows and movies than the likes of Netflix or Disney Plus, but generally...
